spec_match.rationale: Constructor probes are now paired with secure-context, isolated-app scheme, cross-origin-isolated, and relevant permissions-policy gate readouts before the TCPSocket attempt.

interactive.rationale: The open button logs the preflight state and the exact constructor fallback instead of implying Direct Sockets works on a normal origin.

blurb_alignment.rationale: The lede now promises origin, policy, constructor, and attempted connection probes, matching the page.

edge_cases.rationale: The precondition matrix covers origin and policy gates, and the page now includes an error-name map for constructor, security, permission, endpoint, and network failures.

summary: MCP evidence: Chrome 148.0.0.0 loaded http://localhost:3000/v131/direct-sockets-api/direct-sockets/ and showed secure context=yes, IWA URL scheme=http:, crossOriginIsolated=false, direct-sockets policy=not allowed, local-network/loopback-network policy=allowed, direct-sockets-private policy=not allowed, and all socket constructors undefined. The page now includes an error-name map for constructor-undefined, SecurityError, NotAllowedError, NetworkError, and AbortError cases. Clicking open TCPSocket logged the preflight and rejected with Error - TCPSocket not defined. Console had no messages, network requests were 200/204 only, and http://localhost:3000/v131/direct-sockets-api/conformance/ reported 5/5 pass.
